body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

`streaming-partial-writes` (scenario-meanings.md): the assistant message arrives in multiple partial flushes before the turn-end signal; state must stay `working` across all flushes with no premature `â†’ ready`. User-observable signal: `ready â†’ working â†’ ready`, with `working` sustained across every partial flush â€” no flicker to `ready` before the turn-end signal arrives. Primitive `streaming_output`; the irrlicht primitive is that partial flushes do NOT trigger a `â†’ ready` transition. Requires `headless_mode`.

**agent_supports: yes.** Pi streams assistant output incrementally and writes assistant content to the JSONL rollout as it goes. A prompt that elicits a longer reply (e.g. a multi-line haiku or a numbered list of facts) produces several assistant transcript writes within one turn before the final assistant message lands.

**daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready.** Pi's turn boundary is defined ONLY by `stopReason == "stop"`. The pi parser (parser.go:78-83) maps an assistant message with `stopReason == "stop"` to `turn_done` (the workingâ†’ready settle) and EVERY other assistant message â€” including non-terminal partial/streamed flushes â€” to `assistant` (mid-turn), which sustains `working` and is excluded from IsAgentDone(). So streamed/partial assistant writes can never cause a spurious `â†’ ready` flap: the turn is one contiguous `working â†’ ready` arc. The compaction-interspersing risk noted in the stale coverage cell (`daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready`) does NOT apply: pi emits `compaction` as a top-level event that the parser maps to `assistant` (parser.go:55-60), which ALSO sustains `working` (promotes readyâ†’working) rather than settling to ready â€” so even a mid-stream compaction cannot flicker the state. That is why this reassessment lifts the verdict to yes/yes.

**Confidence 0.72** (not higher): you cannot deterministically force a torn/partial JSONL line from a live CLI â€” the assertion here is about state STABILITY across streamed content (one contiguous workingâ†’ready arc, no intermediate ready/working flap), not a hand-crafted oversized/torn line (that is the separate oversized-transcript-line cell). The prompt pins a longer reply so streaming clearly happens, but the exact number of intermediate flushes is model-dependent; only the state topology (single sustained working span, no waiting, one process_exited) is asserted and is robust regardless of flush count.

## Recipe-shape note (SCRIPT / interactive)

Recorded with the INTERACTIVE pi driver (drive-pi-interactive.sh, a tmux REPL) rather than headless `pi --print`. WHY (concern C6): headless `pi --print` exits the instant it emits the final reply, so the daemon's scanner can miss the terminal workingâ†’ready settle before process_exited; script-mode keeps pi alive across one send + wait_turn so the daemon observes the settle cleanly, then a single tmux teardown yields one clean process_exited. The prompt deliberately elicits a LONGER reply (a 6-line haiku, one line at a time) so multiple assistant flushes stream before the turn-end stop. C7 / SESSION-IDENTITY: the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch (it bridges the gap until the UUID-keyed rollout transcript appears), so session_birth â€” which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' â€” legitimately matches that pre-session. The streaming turn arc, pid_bind and teardown all pin to the rollout session via the first-working anchor (NO session pin on turn_start, so the matcher binds the FIRST working, necessarily the rollout session; the pre-session never goes working) + same_session_as. The phase DSL has no per-flush form, so the no-flicker property is asserted as a single sustained working span: exactly ONE workingâ†’ready arc with a no-state_transition-to-ready invariant across the streaming window, plus a no-waiting invariant.
caveats: The whole streamed turn is a SINGLE workingâ†’ready arc: pi sustains working across every partial/streamed assistant flush â€” only the final assistant message with stopReason=stop ends the turn. The spec asserts exactly one turn_end ready, no intermediate ready flap (streaming-window invariant), no waiting.

Pi maps a mid-stream `compaction` top-level event to assistant (sustains working), so the stale coverage caveat 'compaction event interspersing risks flicker' does not hold â€” this is why the verdict is reassessed to yes/yes rather than the coverage cell's irrlicht_observes:partial.
Pi cannot accept a pre-assigned UUID â€” it generates one itself; the interactive driver discovers the UUID from the session header on line 1 of the freshly-created rollout transcript.

Because the live REPL bridges with a transient proc-<PID> pre-session, the turn phase anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher binds the FIRST working (necessarily the rollout session; the pre-session never goes working); every later phase pins to it via same_session_as.

Confidence 0.72: a live CLI cannot be forced to emit a torn/partial JSONL line on demand (that is the oversized-transcript-line cell); the property asserted is state stability across streamed content, and the exact flush count is model-dependent â€” only the state topology is asserted.

sources.note: parser.go:78-83 â€” assistant message with stopReason==stop â†’ turn_done (workingâ†’ready); EVERY other assistant message (incl. streamed/partial flushes) â†’ assistant (mid-turn), sustains working, excluded from IsAgentDone(). parser.go:55-60 â€” top-level `compaction` event â†’ assistant (sustains working), so a mid-stream compaction cannot flicker to ready.

Sibling pi cell confirming the parser sustains working across intermediate assistant transcript writes and settles only on stopReason=stop; events arc is readyâ†’workingâ†’ready with no waiting transition.
streaming-partial-writes: state stays working across all partial flushes â€” no flicker to ready before the turn-end signal arrives; final state ready.
Interactive tmux REPL driver; supports send / wait_turn / interrupt / sleep, keeps the rollout alive so the daemon observes the workingâ†’ready settle before teardown (concern C6).
